changes: OpenClaw shipped a fresh stable release, v2026.5.4, published on 2026-05-05 08:24 UTC after two beta cuts earlier the same day.
Hermes Agent remains canonical in the atlas under NousResearch/hermes-agent, with its latest stable still v2026.4.30; relevant, but unchanged in this run.

PicoClaw, ZeroClaw, AstrBot, and SwarmClaw all still show active repos and recent release cadence, but this run did not surface a new taxonomy shift, breakout launch, or newly validated strategic lane.
whyItMatters: The main signal is that the category anchor shipped again, which reinforces that OpenClaw is still the fastest-moving reference point for the ecosystem.

The rest of the field remains active, but today did not materially change the strategic map: the same five pressures still dominate, lighter runtimes, safer runtimes, multi-agent orchestration, learning loops, and cloud digital workers.
Net: this was a meaningful but narrow run, mostly because the core reference advanced again while the broader competitive structure held steady.
